behaviors.description: The review input is scoped to the lines that changed, not the full content of each modified file.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS applies: this is primarily a data-handoff/orchestration behavior between diff-reading and review-input construction modules. REAL-INFRA/System Triad is not explicit in the behavior text (no required real API contract check or external side-effect readback). LLM-DEP is not required because this checks input scoping, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real diff-parser + review-input builder together on fixture PR diffs containing mixed changed/unchanged hunks, and assert the produced review payload includes only changed-line snippets and excludes unchanged file regions.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA + SYSTEM TRIAD trigger (round-trip externally visible side effect): correctness is that comments appear at correct diff positions in the actual PR UI/API. Mocked placement can pass while real GitHub position semantics fail.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real test PR, post inline comments via the automation, then read back created review comments from GitHub and verify each comment’s commit/path/line (or position fields) maps to intended diff locations.

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a pull request that contains a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP applies directly: correctness depends on model output quality (detecting a real defect). A deterministic stub would hide failure to detect the bug. Workflow not required because the core assertion is a single model judgment outcome, not multi-agentic sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Use a PR/diff fixture with a seeded known bug and run the real model analysis step; assert at least one finding correctly targets the seeded defect category/location.

behaviors.description: Every finding listed in the top-level summary corresponds to a posted inline comment ID from the same run.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS applies: this is cross-module state consistency (inline post results -> summary assembler). System rejected because behavior text does not explicitly require real external contract/round-trip verification; Agentic rejected because mapping integrity does not depend on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute inline-posting module with controlled findings and captured returned comment IDs, then run summary-generation module in the same run context and assert every summary item references an ID present in that run’s posted-inline set.

behaviors.description: No inline finding is dropped from the final summary payload before posting.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS applies: this checks handoff/completeness across modules (finding collection -> summary payload). No explicit REAL-INFRA/System Triad requirement in the text; no LLM-quality dependency required for this completeness invariant.
behaviors.test_description: Feed a known inline-finding set through the real aggregation/serialization pipeline and assert cardinality and stable IDs/keys are preserved in the final summary payload sent to the posting layer.

behaviors.description: The automation sequence (read diff, post inline comments, post summary) succeeds for a real pull request when provided a fixed precomputed finding set.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text explicitly requires a real pull request and end-to-end external effects. SYSTEM TRIAD applies via real external happy-path contract and side-effect visibility (comments/summary actually posted and readable back). Agentic rejected by text: findings are fixed precomputed, so no dependence on model output.
behaviors.test_description: Run the sequence on a real test PR with a fixed finding fixture; assert successful API responses for inline and summary posts and verify via readback that all expected comments and summary content exist on that PR.

behaviors.description: The full live run with model-generated findings (read diff, analyze, post inline comments, post summary) completes correctly for a real pull request without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: This is a full agent pipeline combining real infrastructure and model-generated decisions across sequential stages. MULTI-STEP/agent pipeline criterion applies; failures can emerge from chained agentic-to-system handoffs that single-step Agentic or System tests miss.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the live unattended pipeline on a real test PR using the real model: read diff, generate findings, post inline comments, then post summary; assert pipeline completion plus round-trip verification that posted artifacts are present and internally consistent.
